zh.Montrose, Angus, Scotland: 英國蘇格蘭安格斯蒙特洛斯
zh.Kilmarnock, Ayrshire, Scotland: 蘇格蘭艾斯爾吉爾馬爾諾可

zh.lifeSummary: Hugo Dunkeld Earl van Ross是Euphemia DeBaliol的兒子，於1275年出生在英國蘇格蘭安格斯費亞。他於1308年與Mathilde Bruce在英國蘇格蘭奧可尼結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1333年7月19日在蘇格蘭貝維其可爾享年58歲，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭希倫德。

zh-hans.Fearn, Ross en Cromarty, Schotland: 英国苏格兰安格斯費亞
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Hugo Dunkeld Earl van Ross是Euphemia DeBaliol的儿子，于1275年出生在英国苏格兰安格斯費亞。他于1308年与Mathilde Bruce在英国苏格兰奧可尼结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1333年7月19日在苏格兰貝維其可爾享年58岁，埋葬在英国苏格兰希倫德。

nameMeanings.name: Dunkeld
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: nickname from Middle Low German dunkel, a byform of dunker ‘dark, conceited, unclear’, also applied as a byname for a blind person. Compare Dunckel and Dunkle .

variants.name: Dunckel
meanings: German: variant of   Dunkel  .

variants.name: Dunkle
meanings: Americanized form of German   Dunkel  .

nameMeanings.name: Earl
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English e(o)rl ‘earl’, a traditional English title of nobility equivalent in rank to French and Norman Count .

variants.name: Earle
meanings: English: variant of   Earl  .

variants.name: Harle
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Dieter, Johann, Kurt.
South German (Härle): nickname from a diminutive of Middle High German hār ‘hair’.

English (northern) and Scottish: habitational name from Kirkharle and Little Harle in Northumberland (earlier simply Herle, Harle), possibly named from an Old English personal name Herela (a derivative of various compound names with the first element here ‘army’) + Old English lēah ‘wood, clearing’.

variants.name: Earls
meanings: English: variant of Earl with genitival -s, denoting someone who was a member of an earl's household, or, with post-medieval excrescent -s. In Ireland, when this is not the English name, it is from Gaelic Mac an Iarla ‘son of the earl’.

variants.name: Hearl
meanings: English: variant of Earl with prosthetic H-.

variants.name: Count
meanings: English: nickname from Anglo-Norman French counte ‘count’ (Old French conte, from Latin comes, comitem); a title of French and Norman nobility, ranking as equivalent to English Earl .

variants.name: Earll
meanings: English (Kent): variant of   Earl  .

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Ross
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from one or other of a number of Scottish and English places called Ross or Roos(e), especially Roose (Lancashire) and Roos (East Yorkshire). The placenames derive from a British ancestor of Welsh rhos ‘moor, heath, plain’, which is the sense of the cognate Gaelic word ros. The Scottish surname has at least three origins. First, the Anglo-Norman family from Roos (East Yorkshire) was introduced to Scotland when Robert of Roos, lord of Wark Castle (Northumberland), married Isabella, an illegitimate daughter of King William the Lion. Second, various families took the name from the province of Ross in northern Scotland and other places of that name. Third, there were Norman families in Scotland by the 13th century who probably derived their name from Rots in Normandy (see 2 below). The descendants of Godfrey de Ros, tenant of the de Morville lords of Cunninghame, were major landholders in Ayrshire, and almost certainly took their name from Rots. The Rose family of Kilravock (Nairnshire) may take their name from either of these three (see Rose ). The lairds of Balnagown adopted the surname Ross after the earldom of Ross (to which they considered themselves rightful heirs) had passed into other hands through the female line.

English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Rots in Calvados (France), probably named with the ancient Germanic element rod ‘clearing’ (compare Rhodes ). This was the original home of a family de Ros, who were established in Kent in 1130 and had major estates in Kent until well into the 13th century.

English: habitational name from Wrose, in Shipley, near Bradford (Yorkshire), with re-spelling of Wr- as R- due to the loss of /w/ before /r/ in early modern English pronunciation. The spelling Wrose is no longer current. The placename derives from Old English wrāse ‘knot, something twisted’, referring to the steep-sided hill on which the settlement stands, with the sense ‘broken or twisting hill’.

variants.name: Ruskin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Tauba, Uzi.
English: from the Middle English personal name Rosce (see Ross 4) + the diminutive suffix -kin.
Americanized form of some similar (like-sounding) Jewish (Ashkenazic) surname, for example   Raskin  .

variants.name: Ros
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Enrique, Luis, Jose, Alberto, Eduardo, Rafael, Elena, Fidel, Joaquin, Manuel, Pablo, Ramon.  Southeast Asian Leang, Phon, Lap, Phan, Pho, Saroeun, Sophal, Sophan, Sophea, Tha, Thang, Yen.
Catalan: nickname for someone with blond hair or a fair complexion, ultimately from Latin russus.
Swedish: ornamental name from ros ‘rose’.

variants.name: Rose
meanings: English, Scottish, French, Walloon, Danish, and German: from the name of the flower, Middle English, Old French, Middle High German rose (from Latin rosa), in various applications. In part, it is a topographic name for someone who lived at a place where wild roses grew, or a topographic or habitational name referring to a house bearing the sign of the rose. It is also found, especially in Europe, as a nickname for a man with a ‘rosy’ complexion (compare 4 below). In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates and similar-sounding names from other languages, e.g. Hungarian Rózsa (see Rozsa ), Slovak Róža and Czech Roza . Compare 6 below and French Larose 2.
English: from the Middle English female personal name Rohese, Roese, later Rose, Royse (ancient Germanic Hrodohaidis, Rothaid, composed of the elements hrōd ‘fame, renown’ + haid(is) ‘kind, sort’).
English and Scottish: variant of   Ross  .

variants.name: Rossell
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Anglo-Norman French personal name Ros(c)el, a diminutive of Rosce (see Ross ). Compare the double diminutive Roscelin, with which Ro(s)cel or Ros(s)el was probably freely interchangeable.
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Rosel in Calvados.
English: variant of   Russell  , with which the name in 1 above was easily confused.

variants.name: Rusling
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): patronymic from the Middle English personal name Rocelin (Old French Roscelin, Rocelin, ancient Germanic Ruozelin), a double diminutive of Rozo (see Ross ).

variants.name: Rohs
meanings: German: variant of   Ross  .

variants.name: Rosse
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Dirk, Joris.
German: topographic name from Middle High German rœsse ‘kiln’, in particular one used for drying hemp and flax or for burning lime.
English and Scottish (Lanarkshire): variant of   Ross  .

nameMeanings.name: Hugo
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of   Hugh  , used throughout the Middle Ages in official documents, and now enjoying a revival as a given name.

en.lifeSummary: Hugo Dunkeld Earl van Ross was born in 1275, in Fearn, Angus, Scotland, United Kingdom as the son of Euphemia DeBaliol. He married Mathilde Bruce in 1308, in Orkney, Scotland, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died on 19 July 1333, in Berwickshire, Scotland, at the age of 58, and was buried in Highland, Scotland, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Hugo Dunkeld Earl van Ross est né en 1275, à Fearn, Angus, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, fils d’Euphemia DeBaliol. Il a épousé Mathilde Bruce en 1308, à Orkney, Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé le 19 juillet 1333, à Berwickshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 58 ans, et a été enterré à Highland, Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Hugo Dunkeld Earl van Ross wurde 1275 in Fearn, Angus, Schottland, United Kingdom, als Sohn von Euphemia DeBaliol geboren. Er hat 1308 in Orkney, Schottland, United Kingdom Mathilde Bruce geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb am 19. Juli 1333 im Alter von 58 Jahren in Berwickshire, Scotland und wurde in Highland, Schottland, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Hugo Dunkeld Earl van Ross è nato il nel 1275 nel Fearn, Angus, Scozia, Regno Unito come figlio di Euphemia DeBaliol. Lui sposato Mathilde Bruce nel 1308, nel Orkney, Scozia, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì il 19 luglio 1333, nel Berwickshire, Scotland, all'età di 58 anni, e fu sepolto a Highland, Scozia, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Hugo Dunkeld Earl van Rossは1275年にイギリススコットランドアンガスフエアルンでEuphemia DeBaliolの息子として生まれた。1308年にイギリススコットランドオルクニーでMathilde Bruceと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1333年7月19日にスコットランドバーウィクシュアイアで58歳で死去し，イギリススコットランドヒグhランドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Hugo Dunkeld Earl van Ross(은)는 1275년 영국 스코틀랜드 앤구스 피른에서 Euphemia DeBaliol의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1308년 영국 스코틀랜드 르니에서 Mathilde Bruce(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1333년 7월 19일 스컷랜드 버윅실르에서 58세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드 히랜드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Hugo Dunkeld Earl van Ross nasceu em 1275, em Fearn, Angus, Escócia, United Kingdom filho de Euphemia DeBaliol. Ele casou-se com Mathilde Bruce em 1308, em Orkney, Escócia, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 19 de julho de 1333, em Berwickshire, Scotland, com 58 anos, e foi sepultado em Highland, Escócia, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Hugo Dunkeld Earl van Ross родился в 1275 г. в местности фирн, ангус, Шотландия, Великобритания. Его мать звали Euphemia DeBaliol. Он женился на женщине по имени Mathilde Bruce в 1308 г. в местности орни, Шотландия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер 19 июля 1333 г. в местности бирвиккшир, Шотландия в возрасте 58 лет и был похоронен в местности хаиланд, Шотландия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Hugo Dunkeld Earl van Ross nació en 1275, en Fearn, Angus, Escocia, Reino Unido como hijo de Euphemia DeBaliol. Se casó con Mathilde Bruce en 1308, en Orkney, Escocia, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió el 19 de julio de 1333, en Berwickshire, Scotland, a la edad de 58 años, y fue sepultado en Highland, Escocia, Reino Unido.
